case_name: Mary Margaret Upchurch and Rickie Upchurch v. Adam I. Lewis, M.D. and Jackson Neurosurgery Clinic, PLLC

summary.brief: Mary Margaret Upchurch and Rickie Upchurch v. Adam I. Lewis, M.D. and Jackson Neurosurgery Clinic, PLLC. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Mary Margaret Upchurch suffered paralysis in her legs following a surgery performed at Merit Health Rankin by Dr. Adam Lewis
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A Rankin County Circuit Court jury found that Dr. Lewis was not negligent in his care and treatment of Mary and returned a verdict in favor of Dr. Lewis and Jackson Neurosurgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Beaty concluded that Dr. Lewis 'ended up doing more surgery because he was not prepared.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Beaty

evidence_index.text: Dr. Neely opined that Dr. Lewis did not breach the standard of care owed to Mary in his diagnosis and treatment.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Neely

evidence_index.text: We therefore reverse the circuit court’s judgment and the jury’s verdict in favor of Dr. Lewis and Jackson Neurosurgery and remand the case for a new trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: Mary suffered from severe fibromyalgia and chronic back pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Beaty reiterated that the standard of care additionally required Dr. Lewis to have the proper equipment available.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Beaty

evidence_index.text: Mary’s physician referred her to Dr. Lewis for the replacement of the pain pump.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rickie testified that after the September 2015 surgery to replace the original battery, the spinal cord stimulator finally began providing Mary with noticeable pain relief.
evidence_index.speaker: Rickie Upchurch

evidence_index.text: The jury could have returned a defense verdict simply because it found that the plaintiffs’ experts were not persuasive or credible.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.chief_complaint: Chronic back pain and need for pain pump replacement
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mary Margaret Upchurch had a history of severe fibromyalgia and chronic back pain. She had a spinal cord stimulator and a pain pump implanted previously for pain relief. The pain pump needed replacement.

requestables.name: Pre-surgical consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Lewis regarding pain pump replacement

reveal.result_summary: Discussion about replacing pain pump with Boston Scientific model
reveal.clinical_significance: Informed consent and device compatibility discussion
reveal.decision_impact: Informed consent should have included potential need for lead replacement

timeline_phases.name: Initial Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Consultation with Dr. Lewis for pain pump replacement

timeline_phases.name: Surgical Procedure
timeline_phases.description: Surgery to replace pain pump and battery

key_events.event: Attempt to connect St. Jude leads to Boston Scientific battery

decision_points.clinical_context: During surgery, difficulty connecting St. Jude leads to Boston Scientific battery
decision_points.prompt: What should be done when the lead does not fit the new battery?

options.description: Stop the procedure and consult with patient/family for further consent
options.clinical_reasoning: Ensures informed consent and avoids unnecessary risk

options.description: Proceed with replacing the spinal cord stimulator without additional consent
options.clinical_reasoning: Lack of informed consent for additional procedure

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded with replacing the spinal cord stimulator

expected_action_court.description: Should have stopped and obtained further consent
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Informed consent requires patient awareness of all risks

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Proceeding without explicit consent for additional procedure
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been informed of the need for lead replacement
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Informed consent and standard of care

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered partial paralysis following surgery

key_findings: Breach of standard of care by not obtaining proper consent
Causation established - surgery led to paralysis

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to obtain informed consent for laminectomy
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper consent could have led to different surgical decisions

factual_timeline.event: Patient referred to Dr. Lewis for battery replacement

treatments.treatment: Surgical replacement of pain pump and battery

allegations.allegation: Failure to obtain informed consent for laminectomy

court_findings.finding: Dr. Lewis breached the standard of care
court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to obtain informed consent and use of incompatible equipment

court_findings.finding: Breach of standard of care by not obtaining proper consent
court_findings.legal_significance: Standard of care requires informed consent for significant procedural changes
